---
title: |
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center">
    <img src="logo_b.png" width="350" /><br>
    Milestone Week-8<br>
    <span style="font-size:0.5em;">Mark Kirkpatrick DVM, MS</span>
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet') 

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet')

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |>
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic" &
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |>
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group))

  
# load GC's functions
source('../functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R')
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

# pull solution data

# summary(animals)
# glimpse(animals)
# summary(events_formatted)
# glimpse(events_formatted)
# unique(events_formatted$event_type)
# unique(events_formatted$event)


```


# Report for `r farm_name[[1]]` and `r farm_name[[2]]`


```{r}
#| label: open-html-r
#| eval: false

# 1. Define the file path
file_path <- "images/milestone_09.htm"

# 2. Open the browser
browseURL(file_path)

```



## Describe Herds

-   Describe and compare to the two sample herds using gt table techniques

```{r}
#| label: describe-herds

# =========================================================================
# Initial data summary of deno_cal
# =========================================================================

library(dplyr)

deno_summary <- deno_cal %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(date_time_period_start),
    `Lactation Group` = dplyr::case_when(
      `Lactation Group` == "LACT > 0" ~ "Adults",
      TRUE ~ `Lactation Group`
    )
  ) %>%
  # Grouping order
  # Lactation Group column name contains a space, so it must be referenced with backticks
  group_by(
    year,
    `Lactation Group`,
    location_lact_list
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    ct_animal_days_elig   = sum(ct_animal_days_elig, na.rm = TRUE),
    ct_animals            = sum(ct_animals, na.rm = TRUE),
    ct_animal_lactations  = sum(ct_animal_lactations, na.rm = TRUE),
    days_in_time_period   = sum(days_in_time_period, na.rm = TRUE),
    ct_animal_time_periods = sum(ct_animal_time_periods, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# deno_summary

# =========================================================================
# Apply gt table formatting of summarized data
# =========================================================================

# Add first/last row markers
deno_summary <- deno_summary %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    first_row = dplyr::row_number() == 1,
    last_row  = dplyr::row_number() == dplyr::n()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Build gt table
deno_summary_gt <- deno_summary %>%
  gt(
    groupname_col = "year",
    rowname_col   = "Lactation Group"
  ) %>%
  
  # Hide helper columns
  cols_hide(columns = c(first_row, last_row)) %>%
  
  # Label columns
  cols_label(
    year                    = "Year",
    location_lact_list      = "Herd",
    ct_animal_days_elig     = "Animal Days Eligible",
    ct_animals              = "Animals",
    ct_animal_lactations    = "Animal Lactations",
    days_in_time_period     = "Days in Period",
    ct_animal_time_periods  = "Animal Time Periods"
  ) %>%
  
  # Add spanners for logical grouping
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Counts",
    columns = c(ct_animals, ct_animal_lactations)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Time-Based Metrics",
    columns = c(ct_animal_days_elig, days_in_time_period, ct_animal_time_periods)
  ) %>%
  
  # Format numbers with commas
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(
      ct_animal_days_elig,
      ct_animals,
      ct_animal_lactations,
      days_in_time_period,
      ct_animal_time_periods
    ),
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold column labels
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  # Italic year group labels
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_text(style = "italic")),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold the Lactation Group column values
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = cells_body(columns = "Lactation Group")
  ) %>%
  
  # Shade Herd 2755f rows
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_fill(color = "lightblue")),
    locations = cells_body(rows = location_lact_list == "Herd 2755f")
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold box around YEAR label row
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "all",
      color = "gainsboro",
      weight = px(3)
    ),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold TOP border for first row of each year
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "top",
      color = "gainsboro",
      weight = px(3)
    ),
    locations = cells_body(rows = first_row)
  ) %>%
  
  # Bold BOTTOM border for last row of each year
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "bottom",
      color = "gainsboro",
      weight = px(3)
    ),
    locations = cells_body(rows = last_row)
  ) %>%
  
  # Global table options
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(14),
    row_group.as_column = TRUE,
    data_row.padding = px(6)
  )

deno_summary_gt





```

## Conception Risk Tables

Compare and contrast the sample herds for Heifers and Adult cattle over the years of available data.

```{r}
#| label: create-cr_table

# =========================================================================
# Create conception rate report by herd and lactation group
# =========================================================================

## Explore the data
# unique(events_formatted$location_event)
# explore column names
# colnames(events_formatted)

library(dplyr)
library(gt)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(scales)


# ---- PREP DATA ----

# Add floor_date metrics to events_formatted
events_formatted <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(
    floor_date_month = floor_date(date_event, "month"),
    floor_date_year  = floor_date(date_event, "year")
  )

# Create CR report based on year using fxn_create_CR_reports.
cr_report <- events_formatted |>
  fxn_create_CR_reports(grp_vars = c('location_event', 'lact_group_basic', 'floor_date_year', 'Rstandard'))

cr_clean <- cr_report %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(floor_date_year),
    Lactation = case_when(
      lact_group_basic == "LACT > 0" ~ "Adults",
      TRUE ~ lact_group_basic
    ),
    
    # Assuming source data is already 0-100 scale
    Risk_Scaled = CR, 
    
    # 95% CI with precision = 1
    CI_String = sprintf("%.1f-%.1f", CI_lower, CI_upper)
  ) %>%
  arrange(location_event, lact_group_basic, year)

# ---- FUNCTION TO BUILD ONE TABLE ----
build_cr_table <- function(df) {
  
  subtitle_value <- unique(df$location_event)
  
  df %>%
    select(
      year,
      Lactation,
      Event = `Not Bred Event`,
      Preg = preg,
      Total = total,
      SPC,
      Risk = Risk_Scaled,
      `95% CI` = CI_String,
      CR_bar = Risk_Scaled
    ) %>%
    gt(rowname_col = "year") %>%
    
    cols_label(
      Lactation = "Lactation",
      Event = "Event",
      Preg = "Preg",
      Total = "Total",
      SPC = "SPC",
      Risk = "Risk",
      `95% CI` = "95% CI",
      CR_bar = "Trend"
    ) %>%
    
    tab_header(title = "", subtitle = subtitle_value) %>%
    
    tab_spanner(
      label = "Conception %",
      columns = c(SPC, Risk, `95% CI`)
    ) %>%
    
    fmt_number(columns = c(Event, Preg, Total), decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) %>%
    # Precision = 3 for the Risk and SPC columns in the table body
    fmt_number(columns = c(SPC, Risk), decimals = 3) %>%
    
    # ---- NANOPLOT LOGIC ----
    cols_nanoplot(
      columns = CR_bar,
      plot_type = "bar",
      autoscale = FALSE,
      options = nanoplot_options(
        data_area_fill_color = "orange",
        data_line_stroke_color = "black",
        data_line_stroke_width = 2
      )
    ) %>%
    
    # Visual orange override for nanoplot bar characters
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(color = "orange"),
      locations = cells_body(columns = CR_bar)
    ) %>%
    
    tab_options(
      row.striping.background_color = "gainsboro",
      row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE
    ) %>%
    
    # ---- SUMMARY ROW ----
    grand_summary_rows(
      columns = Risk,
      fns = list(`Location Average` = ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      # Precision = 3 for the Summary Risk
      fmt = list(~ fmt_number(., decimals = 3))
    ) %>%
    
    tab_style(
      style = list(
        cell_fill(color = "#d9e6f2"),
        cell_text(weight = "bold")
      ),
      locations = cells_grand_summary(columns = Risk)
    )
}

# ---- BUILD TABLES ----
tables <- cr_clean %>%
  group_split(location_event, lact_group_basic) %>%
  map(build_cr_table)

# Access tables as:
# tables[[1]]

# Access tables as:
tables[[1]]
tables[[2]]
tables[[3]]
tables[[4]]





```

## Mastitis and Conception Risk

Provided an alternative view of reproductive impacts of MASTITIS in the 1st 60 days and MASTITIS at any time.

```{r}
#| label: mast


# =========================================================================
# Create input files for Kaplan-Meier Survival Analysis
# =========================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(survival)
library(ggsurvfit)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Create variables to report mastitis and pregnancy status
# ---------------------------------------------------------
events_formatted <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(
    mast_60_yn = ifelse(event == "MAST" & dim_event <= 60, 1, 0),
    mast_yn    = ifelse(event == "MAST", 1, 0),
    date_censor = max(date_event, na.rm = TRUE),
    days_open = ifelse(
      !is.na(date_concieved),
      as.numeric(date_concieved - date_fresh),
      as.numeric(date_censor - date_fresh)
    ),
    preg_yn = ifelse(!is.na(date_concieved), 1, 0)
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Adult mastitis events only
# ---------------------------------------------------------
events_mast_adult <- events_formatted %>%
  filter(lact_group_basic != "Heifer",
         event == "MAST")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# FRESH events only (Kaplan–Meier input)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fresh_preg <- events_formatted %>%
  filter(lact_group_basic != "Heifer",
         event == "FRESH")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Collapse mastitis history to one row per animal
# ---------------------------------------------------------
mast_status <- events_mast_adult %>%
  group_by(id_animal_lact) %>%
  summarise(
    mast_60_yn = max(mast_60_yn, na.rm = TRUE),
    mast_yn    = max(mast_yn, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Join mastitis status into fresh_preg
# Keep original values if join returns NA
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fresh_preg <- fresh_preg %>%
  left_join(mast_status, by = "id_animal_lact", suffix = c("_orig", "_mast")) %>%
  mutate(
    mast_60_yn = ifelse(is.na(mast_60_yn_mast), mast_60_yn_orig, mast_60_yn_mast),
    mast_yn    = ifelse(is.na(mast_yn_mast),    mast_yn_orig,    mast_yn_mast)
  ) %>%
  select(-mast_60_yn_orig, -mast_60_yn_mast,
         -mast_yn_orig,    -mast_yn_mast)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Convert mast_yn to factor for KM curves
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fresh_preg <- fresh_preg %>%
  mutate(
    mast_yn = factor(
      mast_yn,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("No Mastitis", "Mastitis")
    )
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Kaplan–Meier model
# ---------------------------------------------------------
km_fit <- survfit(
  Surv(days_open, preg_yn) ~ mast_yn,
  data = fresh_preg
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# ---------------------------------------------------------
surv_plot <- km_fit |>
  ggsurvfit(type = "risk") +
  add_confidence_interval() +
  add_quantile() +
  scale_ggsurvfit() +
  labs(
    x = "Days Open",
    y = "Percent Pregnant",
    title = "Kaplan–Meier Survival: Time to Pregnancy by Mastitis Status"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 300),
    breaks = seq(0, 300, by = 15)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

surv_plot




# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Convert mast_60_yn to factor for KM curves
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fresh_preg <- fresh_preg %>%
  mutate(
    mast_60_yn = factor(
      mast_60_yn,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("No Mastitis", "Mastitis")
    )
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Kaplan–Meier model
# ---------------------------------------------------------
km_fit <- survfit(
  Surv(days_open, preg_yn) ~ mast_60_yn,
  data = fresh_preg
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# ---------------------------------------------------------
surv_plot <- km_fit |>
  ggsurvfit(type = "risk") +
  add_confidence_interval() +
  add_quantile() +
  scale_ggsurvfit() +
  labs(
    x = "Days Open",
    y = "Percent Pregnant",
    title = "Kaplan–Meier Survival: Time to Pregnancy by Mastitis Status"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 300),
    breaks = seq(0, 300, by = 15)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

surv_plot




# # code to help you
# # run this after you've run fxn_disease_status and before you join back to events_formatted
# 
# # your code that runs fxn_dz_status should create this data frame
# select(id_animal_lact = id_animal, location_event, lact_number,
#          mast_60, life_times_mast_60,
#          status_mast_60) |>
#   group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number, mast_60) |>
#   # get last mast mast_60 just using life time ignores mast_60
#   slice_max(order_by = life_times_mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
#   ungroup() |>
#   # now will only end up with 1 row/cow
#   group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number) |>
#   slice_max(order_by = mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
#   ungroup()
# 
# # then join back to events_formatted
 



```
